about.description:
- The following technologies have been used to create this site: Spring boot, Spring Security, Hibernate, PostgreSQL, Liquibase, Angular, Docker, Maven, HTML, CSS. Site is hosted on its own hosting. You can see the code here - https://github.com/GaileAlex/cv.

- During work participated in two projects, where studied and used the following technologies - Java, Spring Boot, Spring Data, Spring Security, Hibernate, PostgreSQL, Liquibase, Docker, Maven, React, Angular.
- Now, in my free time, I study Angular and developing own project.

portfolio.description6:
GoSwift - application for tracking the live queue on the border of Estonia. You can specify your conditions in the queue of vehicles or waiting time, at which point the application will give you a signal. The application collects data from the site https://www.estonianborder.eu/. The Jsoup library is used. Created by JavaFx.
2018.

experience.job-1.description:
- Development of a web application for the collection and analysis of statistical data.
- Solved current tasks on the backend and frontend using the following technologies - Java, Spring Boot, Spring Data, Spring Security, Hibernate, PostgreSQL, Liquibase, Docker, Maven, React, Angular.
- Refactoring existing code to the changing wishes of the client.
- SQL - queries to postgresql database.
- Unit testing using junit, mockito.

travel-log.title: Test Assignment "Travel Log"
travel-log.text:
According to accounting rules travel log should be kept when personal car is used for company needs.

Create an application which has the following functionalities:

● Travel log component(s), where user can:
  ○ Add a new log entry. Each log entry consists of:
    - Date
    - Vehicle registration number
    - Username
    - Odometer value at the beginning of a journey
    - Odometer value at the end of a journey
    Route (i.e. “Tallinn-Rakvere”)
    - Journey short description (i.e. “buying office supplies”)
  ○ View a report based on existing logs. Report is a list of travel log entries that are grouped by date and sorted by odometer initial value. Before generating a report user should be able to customize a report by specifying zero or all of the following filters:
    - Period filter: specific date range (i.e. “01.03.2018 - 01.04.2018”)
    - Vehicle registration number filter
    - Username filter
Also, report should contain a total amount of travelled distance in the specified period.
● Authentication: user authentication is required. Choose one of the following:
  ○ Basic Authentication (username and password)
  ○ OAuth 2.0 (using Google service)
● Authorization:
  ○ Anonymous user should not be able to add or view travel log entries.
  ○ Username of a new travel log entry should be set to a logged-in user name and could not be changed.
  ○ Only an owner of journey log entry (username = logged-in user) can see journey route and description.
